response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional technical depth with 20+ years of software development experience across multiple domains, the role explicitly requires 2 years of people management experience and formal technical leadership—neither of which is evident. This is fundamentally a full-time people-leadership position, and the resume shows exclusively individual contributor roles. Developer tools experience exists but is distributed across multiple positions rather than a focused specialization. The candidate is well-qualified for a senior engineer or architect role but lacks the prerequisite management foundation required.
strengths: 20+ years of software development experience significantly exceeds the 8-year minimum, demonstrating sustained technical growth and career longevity
Hands-on developer tools expertise including CI/CD infrastructure, open-source test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), and current MCP code generator work
Exceptional technical breadth across web, mobile, embedded systems, and DevOps with proficiency in multiple languages and platforms
concerns: Complete absence of demonstrated people management experience—no evidence of managing engineers, conducting performance reviews, or building/leading teams, which is a minimum requirement
Developer tools experience scattered across side projects and brief roles rather than representing a focused 3-year specialization or primary job responsibility
Career pattern shows frequent job transitions (most roles 1-2 years) with no evidence of team leadership, sustained technical mentorship, or organizational change initiatives
